title: Ctenitis manilensis
intro: Ctenitis manilensis, also known as Maniloba fern, is a species of fern native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia, characterized by its delicate fronds and creeping rhizome. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with health benefits attributed to its antidiarrheal, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis manilensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ctenitis manilensis is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly employed in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Ctenitis manilensis is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Ctenitis, which consists of various species of ferns native to different parts of the world. The family Dryopteridaceae, also known as wood ferns, comprises a diverse group of ferns with over 1,200 species worldwide. Ctenitis manilensis is specifically a native species found in certain regions of Asia. This fern is characterized by its unique leaf morphology and growth habits.
